old_claim.note: Micrometers & Precision Measurement provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The corrected node is scoped to repeatable calibration practice for early scientific instruments; micrometers are not retained as a direct prerequisite for that broader practice.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica describes Tycho Brahe's 16th-century astronomical work as using larger, stabler, and better calibrated instruments, before the early-1640s micrometer anchor used by the micrometer node.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Calibrated scientific-instrument practice predates the micrometer node and is not dependent on micrometers.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports calibration as part of earlier astronomical instrument practice, making a direct micrometer prerequisite chronologically and causally too narrow.

ontology_before: Instrument Calibration Standards directly depended on Micrometers & Precision Measurement, making a later precision-measurement node a prerequisite for earlier calibration practice.
ontology_after: Instrument Calibration Standards depends on scientific instrument-making and scientific method, without making micrometers a direct prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-22-renaissance-source-check-scope keeps micrometers_precision_measurement absent as a direct prerequisite for instrument_calibration_standards.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to micrometer-specific calibration standards rather than broader early scientific-instrument calibration.
A source showed early instrument calibration standards directly required micrometers.
short_reason: Calibrated scientific instruments predate the micrometer node, so micrometers are not a direct prerequisite.
